corpus.title: Update 1: Global Nutrition Consortium — Spread Chain
corpus.snippet: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of T...
corpus.content:
Global Nutrition Consortium ANNOUNCES UPDATE: SCIENTIFIC DEVELOPMENTS

A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 6%, controlling for BMI. The announcement comes as the developing scientific story. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.content: Cascade Biotech reported quarterly results in line with analyst expectations. Revenue of $407M was up 7.2% year-over-year. CEO remarked conditions remain favourable heading into the next quarter.

corpus.title: Sugar Intake and Diabetes Risk Meta-analysis
corpus.snippet: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for B...
corpus.content: High sugar intake was associated with a 22% increased diabetes risk after adjusting for BMI.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — Global Nutrition Consortium
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of Global Nutrition Consortium data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — Global Nutrition Consortium
Date: 2024-02-25

This record confirms A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for BMI. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.

corpus.title: News: Global Nutrition Consortium — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22...
corpus.content:
Global Nutrition Consortium ANNOUNCES KEY SCIENTIFIC UPDATE

A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for BMI. The announcement comes as changes in the scientific sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.
